records.ety: [L. excedere, excessum, to go away or beyond; ex out + cedere to go, to pass: cf. F. excder. See Cede.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exceeded; p. pr. & vb. n. Exceeding.]

defs: To go beyond; to proceed beyond the given or supposed limit or measure of; to outgo; to surpass; -- used both in a good and a bad sense; as, one man exceeds another in bulk, stature, weight, power, skill, etc.; one offender exceeds another in villainy; his rank exceeds yours.
quotes: Name the time, but let it not Exceed three days.
Observes how much a chintz exceeds mohair.

defs: To go too far; to pass the proper bounds or measure.
quotes: Forty stripes he may give him, and not exceed.

defs: To be more or greater; to be paramount.
